name: winapp
description: CLI for Windows app development, including package identity, packaging, managing appxmanifest.xml, test certificates, Windows (App) SDK projections, and more. For use with any app framework targeting Windows

--cli-schema.description: Output the complete CLI command structure as JSON for tooling, scripting, and LLM integration. Includes all commands, options, arguments, and their descriptions.

cert.description: Manage development certificates for code signing. Use 'cert generate' to create a self-signed certificate for testing, or 'cert install' (requires elevation) to trust an existing certificate on this machine.

generate.description: Create a self-signed certificate for local testing only. Publisher must match AppxManifest.xml (auto-inferred if --manifest provided or appxmanifest.xml is in working directory). Output: devcert.pfx (default password: 'password'). For production, obtain a certificate from a trusted CA. Use 'cert install' to trust on this machine.

--export-cer.description: Export a .cer file (public key only) alongside the .pfx

--if-exists.description: Behavior when output file exists: 'error' (fail, default), 'skip' (keep existing), or 'overwrite' (replace)

--install.description: Install the certificate to the local machine store after generation

--manifest.description: Path to appxmanifest.xml or Package.appxmanifest file to extract publisher information from

--output.description: Output path for the generated PFX file

--password.description: Password for the generated PFX file

--publisher.description: Publisher name for the generated certificate. If not specified, will be inferred from manifest.

--valid-days.description: Number of days the certificate is valid

info.description: Display certificate details (subject, thumbprint, expiry). Useful for verifying a certificate matches your manifest before signing.

install.description: Trust a certificate on this machine (requires admin). Run before installing MSIX packages signed with dev certificates. Example: winapp cert install ./devcert.pfx. Only needed once per certificate.

cert-path.description: Path to the certificate file (PFX or CER)

--force.description: Force installation even if the certificate already exists

create-debug-identity.description: Enable package identity for debugging without creating full MSIX. Required for testing Windows APIs (push notifications, share target, etc.) during development. Example: winapp create-debug-identity ./myapp.exe. Requires appxmanifest.xml in current directory or passed via --manifest. Re-run after changing appxmanifest.xml or Assets/.

entrypoint.description: Path to the .exe that will need to run with identity, or entrypoint script.

--keep-identity.description: Keep the package identity from the manifest as-is, without appending '.debug' to the package name and application ID.

--no-install.description: Do not install the package after creation.

create-external-catalog.description: Generates a CodeIntegrityExternal.cat catalog file with hashes of executable files from specified directories. Used with the TrustedLaunch flag in MSIX sparse package manifests (AllowExternalContent) to allow execution of external files not included in the package.

input-folder.description: List of input folders with executable files to process (separated by semicolons)

--compute-flat-hashes.description: Include flat hashes when generating the catalog

--if-exists.description: Behavior when output file already exists

--output.description: Output catalog file path. If not specified, the default CodeIntegrityExternal.cat name is used.

--use-page-hashes.description: Include page hashes when generating the catalog

get-winapp-path.description: Print the path to the .winapp directory. Use --global for the shared cache location, or omit for the project-local .winapp folder. Useful for build scripts that need to reference installed packages.

--global.description: Get the global .winapp directory instead of local

init.description: Start here for initializing a Windows app with required setup. Sets up everything needed for Windows app development: creates appxmanifest.xml with default assets, creates winapp.yaml for version management, and downloads Windows SDK and Windows App SDK packages and generates projections. Interactive by default (use --use-defaults to skip prompts). Use 'restore' instead if you cloned a repo that already has winapp.yaml. Use 'manifest generate' if you only need a manifest, or 'cert generate' if you need a development certificate for code signing.

base-directory.description: Base/root directory for the winapp workspace, for consumption or installation.

--config-dir.description: Directory to read/store configuration (default: current directory)

--config-only.description: Only handle configuration file operations (create if missing, validate if exists). Skip package installation and other workspace setup steps.

--ignore-config.description: Don't use configuration file for version management

--setup-sdks.description: SDK installation mode: 'stable' (default), 'preview', 'experimental', or 'none' (skip SDK installation)

--use-defaults.description: Do not prompt, and use default of all prompts

manifest.description: Create and modify appxmanifest.xml files for package identity and MSIX packaging. Use 'manifest generate' to create a new manifest, 'manifest update-assets' to regenerate app icons, or 'manifest add-alias' to add an execution alias.

add-alias.description: Add an execution alias (uap5:AppExecutionAlias) to an appxmanifest.xml. This allows launching the packaged app from the command line by typing the alias name. By default, the alias is inferred from the Executable attribute (e.g. $targetnametoken$.exe becomes $targetnametoken$.exe alias).

--app-id.description: Application Id to add the alias to (default: first Application element)

--manifest.description: Path to AppxManifest.xml or Package.appxmanifest file (default: search current directory)

--name.description: Alias name (e.g. 'myapp.exe'). Default: inferred from the Executable attribute in the manifest.

generate.description: Create appxmanifest.xml without full project setup. Use when you only need a manifest and image assets (no SDKs, no certificate). For full setup, use 'init' instead. Templates: 'packaged' (full MSIX), 'sparse' (desktop app needing Windows APIs).

--description.description: Human-readable app description shown during installation and in Windows Settings

--executable.description: Path to the application's executable. Default: <package-name>.exe

--if-exists.description: Behavior when output file exists: 'error' (fail, default), 'skip' (keep existing), or 'overwrite' (replace)

--template.description: Manifest template type: 'packaged' (full MSIX app, default) or 'sparse' (desktop app with package identity for Windows APIs)

update-assets.description: Generate new assets for images referenced in an appxmanifest.xml from a single source image. Source image should be at least 400x400 pixels.

image-path.description: Path to source image file (SVG, PNG, ICO, JPG, BMP, GIF)

--light-image.description: Path to source image for light theme variants (SVG, PNG, ICO, JPG, BMP, GIF)

--manifest.description: Path to AppxManifest.xml or Package.appxmanifest file (default: search current directory)

package.description: Create MSIX installer from your built app. Run after building your app. A manifest (appxmanifest.xml or package.appxmanifest) is required for packaging - it must be in current working directory, passed as --manifest or be in the input folder. Use --cert devcert.pfx to sign for testing. Example: winapp package ./dist --manifest appxmanifest.xml --cert ./devcert.pfx

--cert.description: Path to signing certificate (will auto-sign if provided)

--executable.description: Path to the executable relative to the input folder.

--manifest.description: Path to AppX manifest file (default: auto-detect from input folder or current directory)

--output.description: Output msix file name for the generated package (defaults to <name>.msix)

--self-contained.description: Bundle Windows App SDK runtime for self-contained deployment

restore.description: Use after cloning a repo or when .winapp/ folder is missing. Reinstalls SDK packages from existing winapp.yaml without changing versions. Requires winapp.yaml (created by 'init'). To check for newer SDK versions, use 'update' instead.

base-directory.description: Base/root directory for the winapp workspace

--config-dir.description: Directory to read configuration from (default: current directory)

run.description: Creates packaged layout, registers the Application, and launches the packaged application.

input-folder.description: Input folder containing the app to run

--args.description: Command-line arguments to pass to the application

--debug-output.description: Capture OutputDebugString messages and first-chance exceptions from the launched application. Only one debugger can attach to a process at a time, so other debuggers (Visual Studio, VS Code) cannot be used simultaneously. Use --no-launch instead if you need to attach a different debugger. Cannot be combined with --no-launch or --json.

--manifest.description: Path to the appxmanifest.xml (default: auto-detect from input folder or current directory)

--no-launch.description: Only create the debug identity and register the package without launching the application

--output-appx-directory.description: Output directory for the loose layout package. If not specified, a directory named AppX inside the input-folder directory will be used.

--unregister-on-exit.description: Unregister the development package after the application exits. Only removes packages registered in development mode.

--with-alias.description: Launch the app using its execution alias instead of AUMID activation. The app runs in the current terminal with inherited stdin/stdout/stderr. Requires a uap5:ExecutionAlias in the manifest. Use "winapp manifest add-alias" to add an execution alias to the manifest.

sign.description: Code-sign an MSIX package or executable. Example: winapp sign ./app.msix ./devcert.pfx. Use --timestamp for production builds to remain valid after cert expires. The 'package' command can sign automatically with --cert.

file-path.description: Path to the file/package to sign

cert-path.description: Path to the certificate file (PFX format)

store.description: Run a Microsoft Store Developer CLI command. This command will download the Microsoft Store Developer CLI if not already downloaded. Learn more about the Microsoft Store Developer CLI here: https://aka.ms/msstoredevcli

tool.description: Run Windows SDK tools directly (makeappx, signtool, makepri, etc.). Auto-downloads Build Tools if needed. For most tasks, prefer higher-level commands like 'package' or 'sign'. Example: winapp tool makeappx pack /d ./folder /p ./out.msix

unregister.description: Unregisters a sideloaded development package. Only removes packages registered in development mode (e.g., via 'winapp run' or 'create-debug-identity').

--force.description: Skip the install-location directory check and unregister even if the package was registered from a different project tree

--manifest.description: Path to the appxmanifest.xml (default: auto-detect from current directory)

update.description: Check for and install newer SDK versions. Updates winapp.yaml with latest versions and reinstalls packages. Requires existing winapp.yaml (created by 'init'). Use --setup-sdks preview for preview SDKs. To reinstall current versions without updating, use 'restore' instead.

--setup-sdks.description: SDK installation mode: 'stable' (default), 'preview', 'experimental', or 'none' (skip SDK installation)
